name: Mrs. Mae Landers Adams
description: Mrs. Adams has managed to complete her college course without attending during the regular session, so we have missed the pleasure of knowing her, for which we are sorry.

name: Sara Clementine Allen
description: Clem has a cordial, genial nature which endears her to the whole campus, and with it a dignity which compels respect as her personality compels love. Clem graduates in three years with a splendid record.

name: Lizzie Lee Allsup
description: Besides making a brilliant record as a student and graduating in an astonishingly short time, Lizzie Lee has distinguished herself in literary work. She is a member of Chi Delta Phi, and has served in numerous capacities on the Crimson and Entre-Nous.

name: Jessie Lee Ansley
description: Jessie Lee holds an undisputed place among our best read and deepest thinking students. She is the true scholar. It is unnecessary to say that she has always made the honor roll and that she has been voted the most intellectual girl on the campus.

name: Bivien Hunter Awtrey
description: What phrase could characterize Bill better than, "A smile will go a long, long way"? She will always be remembered as an ideal girl, and as one who does cheerfully what she considers her duty. Her poise and dignity are only the veneer of a delightfully warm, frank and unreserved nature. She is "efficiency" down to her finger tips. As editor-in-chief of the Entre Nous, Bill has proved herself an executive of no small ability. Three years has been the length of her stay in college. How has she done it all?

name: A. L. Bains
description: One can hardly imagine Howard's campus without the decorative presence of Al. He's truly Howard's Greek god, and the entire student body recognized it by vote. Al has been most active in athletics, and the baseball team will be without a sensational fielder and captain.

name: Alton Barton
description: Barton has few rivals in his hold on the affections of a large part of the student body. Vice-president of our class, he is the capable B. Y. P. U. director at Ruhama, the college church, a talented musician and a plucky and valuable man on the football field, though he weighs only 135. Charming, too, is he, and we hate to see him go, though we know it is to greater honors still.

name: J. Newton Bass
description: Newton is everybody's friend and everybody's Newton's friend. Making college in three years is a big job, and it takes a big man to do it. Newton also went to Cuba with the naval reserves, but he went at a different time. I wonder if it was to keep those Spanish senoritas from thinking he was George.

name: J. George Bass
description: "A man's man," who has the ability to make everybody like him. George is a friend, a helper, a jolly good fellow. He wouldn't have hurried through college in three years. George went to Cuba last summer with the naval reserves. We hear he had a "tiempo de gran."

name: Frances Bohannon
motto: "Never idle a moment, but thrifty and thoughtful of others."
description: Bo has enthusiasm for whatever she does—a sort of do-it-or-die spirit that makes her accomplishments many and varied. She is a good student, graduating in three years—the youngest member in our class.

name: Ethel Bond
description: Ethel has had a hand in quite a number of student activities, especially religious ones. She is a girl of poise and geniality besides, and brains which combined make her an excellent student.

name: Ernestine Bondurant
description: Ernestine's talents tend toward art and student work. In both she is very efficient, and with all her capabilities she has a spontaneous smile and a happy sunshiny nature. She is graduating in three years.

name: Floy Mignon Boyd
description: Our French dictionary says "mignon" means "small, dainty, fairy-like." Floy's name is indeed appropriate. Although she went to Woman's College and Birmingham-Southern, she picked Howard for her Alma Mater.

name: Mamie Broughton
description: We are glad Mamie decided to take her degree at Howard after attending three years at Judson. While there, among other things, she took her "Home Ec." diploma and was on the "Y" cabinet. Though she is somewhat reserved, those who seek Mamie soon find her very amiable and dependable.

name: William Nutt Burchfiel
description: Billie is a true gentleman, cordial and courteous. He has found time to do steady classwork and keep in the thick of student life, and at the same time hold down a responsible position at Doster-Northington, Inc.

name: L. Earl Carroll
clubs: State Y. M. C. A. Council (3)
President State Y. M. C. A. Council (4)
Member of Y. M. C. A. Council for South
description: A list of Carroll's honors and activities bear witness to his superior executive ability and poise, as well as the high esteem of his fellow students. Having more than most anyone to do, still he makes honor roll. He could not be praised too much, and there is no doubt of his success as a "Y" worker.

name: Alfred Major Chilton
description: Major is Knox Spearman's twin. Brainy and hard working, he is frequently seen about the Science Hall. Besides a splendid college record he has done considerable outside work.

name: Dorothy Evelyn Clark
clubs: Best All-Round Girl in Freshman Class
description: Evelyn has been the mainstay of the Girls' Glee Club since her freshman year. She is of pleasing appearance and is the finest sort of pal to have.

name: Jack Coker
description: Jack has accomplished much during her three years at Howard. She has made the Honor Roll each year, served the Glee Club as an accompanist and as a singer, and is a member of the Allied Arts Club.

name: J. E. Dillard
description: Besides being our star cornetist, Jim possesses a brilliant mind and a most engaging personality. With the start he has he bids fair to succeed a la his renowned father.

name: Ruby Deane Doyle
description: Ruby Deane is one of those poor unfortunate workers in the registrar's office whom we puzzled folk worry with our questions. Ruby Deane, in spite of the fact that she is graduating in three years, found time to earn the A. D. Smith Improvement Medal, make Honor Roll and Beta Pi Theta.

name: B. F. Dykes
description: Dykes is one of those divines and carries heavy pastoral and family responsibilities. With his conscientiousness and ability, he should succeed in his chosen work. Ask him about Texas!

name: Vera Ellard
description: That she is finishing in three years attests Vera's persistence and excellence in her studies. A local girl, she is a lovely character and has many strong friends.

name: Alfred H. Eubank
motto: "For e'en tho' vanquished, he could argue still."
description: Alf, during his four years at Howard, has always been in the thick of things. Drama, journalism, parades and politics are just a few of the activities that have claimed his attention.

name: Sarah Fausett
description: Though completing her course in three years, Sarah is a leader in several fields. She has sung in the Girls' Glee Club from the first, was a charter member and secretary of the Allied Arts Club in the art department, and in her senior year has been biology lab instructor. One may also mention her striking personality, her love affair, and her wide circle of friends and acquaintances.

name: J. E. Franks
description: Mr. Franks is a zealous worker for the cause of Christianity and Christian education, and we predict for him a great success in whatever line of work he may undertake.

name: Sadie Gardner
description: Sadie has been at Howard only three years. We are glad that so dependable and loyal a girl has hastened her work a bit to graduate with us.

name: Pollie Jane Garrett
motto: "A violet by a mossy stone Half hidden from the eye!"
description: Pollie is very quiet and demure. She is a sincere worker in religious activities on and off the campus.

name: Mildred Gantt
description: Mildred holds such high standards before herself that she is disappointed if she receives one B. This has happened very seldom since Mildred entered Howard College.

name: Willie Earle Gibbs
description: Yes, the name is in the right place. In spite of her masculine name, Willie is a womanly girl that has made a big place in the affections and esteem of both faculty and students during the three years it has taken her to get her degree.

name: R. E. Glenn
description: Glenn has a ready smile which betrays a good disposition and wins many friends. He is steady and capable and will make his mark.

name: Paul Gourley
description: Paul's good looks and ways with the ladies have served him well in several plays as well as on the campus. He knows the worth of work and dependability, too, and should be a success where he chooses

name: Effie Mae Hacker
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer B. S. U. (4)
description: Those who call Effie Mae "friend" are fortunate. She is an active worker in religious circles, is an "A" student, and one of Howard's most loyal supporters.

name: Julian A. Hayes
description: Julian's broad smile is known to all Howardites. He has made a splendid scholastic record. He is interested in folks, and withal, as a ministerial student has proven most outstanding in religious activities.

name: James Luther Hearn
clubs: Chairman Finance Committee and Float Committee (4)
description: Luther is the best known and best liked man on the campus. He is very versatile; he sings and has served the college publications as business manager of the Entre Nous and as editor of the Crimson. He is historian of the Senior Class.

name: Millard Fillmore Hearn
description: Millard may be best described as "collegiate." A meticulous dresser, a perfect ladies' man, and well liked among the men as well, he is a perfect example of the typical college man.

name: Ruby Hollingsworth
description: Ruby is Professor Eagles' right-hand man. She is high priestess of the Temple of Mathematics. In her freshman year she won a math. medal. Now she is majoring in math., minoring in physics, and besides that is graduating in three years!

name: Claudie Mae Hoover
motto: “She is loved for her smile, her look, Her way of speaking gently.”
description: Sammie's happy-go-lucky nature, frank smile, humor and never-failing pep endear her to the entire student body. We must not overlook the fact that Sammie is finishing in three years.

name: J. O. Johnston
description: Mr. Johnston entered college late in life, but he has certainly shown ability and conscientiousness in his college work in spite of family and pastoral responsibilities.

name: Mary Elizabeth Keller
description: Mary is one of those students whose grades would spell happiness for many a less talented person. A shark at math., along with other things, she looks as though life is pleasant to her.

name: Aaron Kendrick
description: Aaron is a steady, consistent worker in all he does, and wins the approval of all with whom he comes in contact.

name: John Kendrick
description: Kendrick is good-natured and a good student, especially in botany. No one has more thoroughly won his diploma.

name: Malla Jane Ketcham
description: "The Girl with the Smile" would be a fitting title for Malla Jane. She is one of our prettiest and most popular co-eds and a moving force in campus activities.

name: Mrs. Mary Clo Lasster
clubs: Winner A. D. Smith Math Improvement Medal (3)
description: The earnestness and sincerity which characterizes Mary Clo has been responsible for a large part of her success. She is a conscientious student and a good worker.

name: W. L. Lenderman, A.B.
description: During his first two years at Howard Bill was well known, but returning after an interval, he seems to have turned serious and attends strictly to his own biz. Anyone who knows him, though, will readily tell you what a fine man he is.

name: Luther E. Little, A.B.
description: Always well groomed and immaculate, Luther is a man of charming personality and ready friendship. Quiet and unobtrusive, he has been a force among his fellow students.

name: John Hope Lollar, A.B.
description: Slick is one of the five men on Howard's football team this year who will graduate without seeing Howard defeated by Birmingham-Southern. He is one of the most popular men in the class, as is shown by his being elected class president. For him there are other worlds to conquer, and we hope and believe he will conquer them.

name: Lora Lee Longshore, A.B.
description: A pleasing combination of brains and beauty is Lora. She is characterized by her sympathy, warm generous nature and her friendliness. She is in such haste to get back home that she has shortened her stay at Howard.

name: J. Frank Lowery, A.B.
description: Chink hails from Oneonta, Alabama, the home of so many of our athletes. He has played on the basketball and baseball teams during his stay with us, and has held the very enviable position of coach of the girls' basketball team.

name: Waldine Mason, A.B.
motto: "She sits in a mystery calm and intense."
enrolltime: From Judson Waldine comes to graduate at Howard.
description: Waldine attended Judson her freshman year. From Judson Waldine comes to graduate at Howard. She has a friendly, sweet and unaffected nature.

name: Addilee Mattison, A.B.
description: Jimmie wins us with her magnetic personality, charms us with her delightful voice, and we love her for herself.

name: Quintus Isaac Mayton, A.B.
motto: "The glory of a firm, capacious mind."
description: Mr. Mayton is an assiduous worker in religious fields, and is well liked by his associates.

name: Mildred Merchant, A.B.
description: Mildred is a girl of many virtues. She is genial, a thoroughly sympathetic friend, a good student and endowed with nature's greatest gift—"love."

name: J. Rogers Moore, A.B.
clubs: Chairman Literary Department of Allied Arts Club
officer in B. Y. P. U.
description: Moore was something of an athlete during his two years at Auburn, so he has had to confine his prowess to tennis at Howard. He is an outstanding personage, especially in literary work and in church work, having made Sigma Upsilon, Allied Arts and Crimson staff, and being an officer in both B. Y. P. U. and in Sunday school.

name: A. G. Mullins, A.B.
description: Mullins is one of our most promising young ministers. Membership in Sigma Upsilon is recognition of his superior literary work, and besides that he takes a keen interest in class work and in other student activities.

name: Pauline McCown, A.B.
description: Pauline will graduate with honors, for she has acted as marshal for her class for two years, is a member of Beta Pi Theta, and has one of Professor DeLauney's stars and a math. medal to her credit. She is a "carrot-top," but a refutation of the theory that people with red hair have high tempers.

name: Willie Kathleen McCormack
description: Bill is a lovely, friendly character, who always leaves one better for having come in contact with her. Bill has not been with us during the whole year, but we are happy to have had her company for the last semester.

name: Morgan Cooper Nichols
description: The fact that Joe hid himself at Auburn for more than a year could not keep him from being one of the most popular men on the campus at Howard. For two years he has been a cheer leader. He's a man's man, as well as a ladies' man, and a splendid, though modest character.

name: Lessye Nelson
description: We are sure that when we are close to Lessye with the mystic "Parlez-vous francais?" she will be able to respond with the correct pass word, for she is a member of Le Cercle Francais.

name: Valeria Pippin
description: Valeria has done most of her work in extension, so we haven't known her until this year. She is a girl of fine ideals and is not afraid of work.

name: Jesse Grant Porter
description: Porter's solid worth is comparable to his splendid physique, which has served the Bullpups many a good turn in an earlier year. He is most likable.

name: James Howard Putman
description: Jimmie, in his college career, has accomplished a great deal. A biology instructor, a great B. Y. P. U. worker, he is a fine all-round boy, and his many friends will miss his cheery presence.

name: Cecile Reeves
description: "Sis" is a Senior who has a real force of character that makes her a valuable part of our class.

name: H. C. Rogers
clubs: State B. Y. P. U. Worker
Representative to State B. S. U. (2, 3, 4)
President of Alabama B. S. U. (4)
College Secretary to B. S. U. (2, 3, 4)
President of Interdenominational Council of Religious Education for Alabama (3, 4)
Second Vice-President of Religious Education Club (4)
Plans and Program Committee All-Southern Baptist Student Conference (4)
description: He lays no claim to being a politician, but he has served very competently as an officer in many of the student activities. He is one of the most energetic of our students, and is a whole-hearted worker in religious activities.

name: Earl T. Rogers
description: Different from the rest, calm, cool and indifferent, Earl has the admiration and friendship of all who know him. Under his quietness there lurks an imp of wit and humor, and he is indeed pleasant to know.

name: George Merritt Rogers
motto: "What's the use of worrying? It never was worth while."
description: "George" is a synonym for a jolly good sport and a really and truly Pollyanna disposition. "What's the use of worrying?" If you want to know anything, ask questions and when in doubt, laugh.

name: J. Lawton Rogers
description: Lawton is a hard worker, one of the kind who always succeeds in what he undertakes. We predict for him a future of unbroken success.

name: Vera Scott
description: Vera is a volunteer for Christian life service and has shaped her college course with this end in view. We are justly proud of this fair product of Howard College and we are expecting great things of her in the future.

name: Arnold Seale
description: Arnold's cheery nature and jovial friendliness are two of the traits that have won for him the honor of being among the most popular students. Uncomplaining, though his thesis was lost in the fire, he set to work again, and experts in the field say that it is a masterly study.

name: Olin T. Smith
description: A crack basketeer, Olin has played a stellar role on Howard's cage floor for four years. His bonny disposition and dependability have won many staunch friends and acquaintances.

name: Knox Spearman
description: Knox stars in Howard's journalistic circles. He is splendid in English and Science, yet not found wanting in any line, and especially not in disposition, for everyone likes this quiet fellow.

name: Edith Mary Southard
motto: "A little learning is a dangerous thing."
description: Edith took warning for she has emulated Minerva. She is proof that a girl can be both attractive and capable. Capability, broad-mindedness, sympathy—that's what friendship with her shows.

name: Elizabeth Stephenson
motto: "Her happy laugh and cheerful manner brighten all sorts of weather."
description: Mannerisms, magnetism, eyes of blue inviting make up Betty's dainty charm. Betty has style and poise about her.

name: Norman P. Teague
description: Norman entered Howard as one of the greenest rats that ever bowed before a Sophomore's paddle; since that time he has learned the ways of a college man. He is a very popular member of the band.

name: Hubert Mace Thomas
description: Mr. Thomas is one of a number who has combined ministerial duties with those of securing an education, and in both occupations has stood with the foremost. We think that he will also be able to cope with the larger and more difficult problems of life.

name: John Bernard Vaughn
description: Vaughn is a well known figure on the campus. Anything about Howard interests him, and he is faithful and steady, as well as likable.

name: Mildred Louise Wall
description: Louise was out of school for a year or so, and is consequently not so well known among the members of the Class of '27. Nevertheless, she has numerous friends who appreciated her fine dependability and lovable personality.

name: Frank Thomas Walsh
description: Butch is indeed a friend. Although he spent his first year at Auburn, he soon fell into line and acquired a large share of that intangible something commonly called "Howard spirit." Butch has made for himself a host of friends among the members of our student body and we look upon him as an indispensable part of our class.

name: Clyde Thomas Warren
motto: ". . . and still the wonder grew That one small head could carry all he knew."
description: Clyde has maintained such a high scholarship during his four years at Howard and has proved himself so capable in every phase of college life that the record he leaves is a remarkable one. Clyde has the distinction of being the first to receive the Honors Degree from Howard.

name: Susan Aline Sargent
description: Who doesn't remember the laughs Sue gave us her freshman year as the black-faced comedian in Pat's Glee Club? She has proved herself a good sport and a true supporter of Howard and Pete. We all like her.

name: W. A. Wiggins
description: The profs surely like to have Mr. Wiggins around, for he does such steady, splendid work, and besides shows such keen interest. He radiates good spirits, and we prophesy success in the ministry for him.

name: Dorothy Youngblood
motto: "Pretty, witty, original, wise; A thousand girls in one."
description: Dorothy's looks and talents make her one of Howard's most distinguished Seniors. This year she did such work on the Crimson staff that the Senior Class elected her Senior Editor of the Entre Nous. She is also a member of the art group of the Allied Arts Club. We predict a great future for Dorothy in the literary field.

name: Mrs. Letha Hardy Birchfield
description: We scarcely know her, for she has done her work in extension, which is indeed our misfortune; but we are happy that she is to graduate with us, and that we have had her company for even this short while.

name: Mary Ella Thomas
description: There's scarcely a student more in demand than Ella, and the secret of it—her lovely voice. We are quite proud of her. She sings over radio and everywhere in Birmingham. She often brightens dull chapels by singing for us.

name: Fletcher Spicer
description: Not only is he proficient in the art of bucking the line, but as captain this year he has smashed hearts as well. He does not look like a ladies' man, but his actions betray him, and he adds many foolish virgins' scalps to his belt. Bull is a good reliable football man, and will be remembered as such.

name: Edwin Lamberth
description: A good natured, lazy fellow, and liked by the entire college for his indifference. He has been quite active in social circles and absolutely does not allow studying to interfere with his college course.

name: Claude Matthews Brewster
description: Claude is a friendly, likable chap who plays football and has a jolly good time. He is one of the players who has never been defeated by Southern. He also is quite sociable, and was a member of the famous "Mask Club."

name: John L. Johnson
description: John possesses what most folks don't. He has the remarkable power of oratory, and has used it for Howard on several occasions. We predict a great future for John L.

name: Russell Tinklepaugh
description: "Little Tink" is finishing this year and will leave a huge gap to be filled by somebody. It has been a number of years since "Big Tink" came, and now even "Little Tink" is going out. We are assured of "Tink's" success in business, as he is the efficient chairman of the senior ring committee, and when a man can boss seniors he is indeed a good man.

name: Albert Hayden McDanal
description: Hayden is a gentleman. A thoroughly liked and corking good sport. Nothing is dull as long as Hayden is about. He is another of those lucky football players who has never bowed in defeat before Southern.

name: Alexander Stewart Smith
description: A. S. is a hard working student who will graduate with knowledge of his subject as well as a degree. A dependable man, always on time with his work, and deeply respected by the faculty and entire student body.

name: Lucile Higgins
clubs: Undergraduate Representative Y. W. C. A.

name: JEWEL BURNHAM
clubs: Etowah County W. M. U. Scholarship

name: JOHN R. RUSHING
clubs: President College B. Y. P. U.
